_provenance.stage: Stage 6 — Synthesis (retroactive rebuild from Codex Stage 3)
version_history: v2 (2026-05-17): retroactive editorial pass. Stage 3 (Codex) FAS=58 with 7 verified claims. Key corrections: 'stranded' → 'awaiting clearance' per BERNAMA; transit happened in stages (only 2 of 7 had transited by mid-April). 'Malaysia has no deployed naval assets' (UNVERIFIED gap) → 'no RMN naval escort was required or deployed'. Japan SDF clarification: mission area excludes Strait of Hormuz (Stage 3 fact-correction). '~2,000 ships blocked globally' (INCORRECT — UN/IMO verified ~20,000 seafarers, not '2,000 ships') corrected. 'Malaysia banned US warships' (UNVERIFIED) softened to 'reported to stop accepting port-of-call applications'. 'Malaysia chose neither, gap showed' (analytical assertion) replaced with sourced 'CMF membership without operational presence' framing.

revision_log: CORRECTED (Stage 3): MISLEADING — 'stranded' → 'awaiting clearance' (BERNAMA: vessels were not detained).
CORRECTED (Stage 3): MISLEADING — Hook 'cleared to pass' → 'approved... transit happened in stages' (Stage 3: by mid-April only 2 of 7 had transited).
CORRECTED (Stage 3): UNVERIFIED — 'no deployed naval assets in the Gulf' → 'no RMN naval escort was required or deployed for these vessels' (Stage 3 verified the latter, not the broader gap claim).
CORRECTED (Stage 3): MISLEADING — 'Japan runs an SDF mission near Hormuz' → Japan's MOD says mission area explicitly excludes the Strait of Hormuz.
REPHRASED (Stage 3): MISLEADING — 'Malaysia contributes nothing operational' dropped (broader than evidence).
REPHRASED (Stage 3): UNVERIFIED — 'Higher crude boosts Petronas revenue' qualified to 'can raise Petronas revenue and add to fuel subsidy outlay simultaneously, depending on hedging and assumptions'.
REPHRASED (Stage 3): UNVERIFIED — 'RM4B subsidy and rising costs land on B40 households, hawkers, fishers and rural commuters' dropped specific distributional claim.
CORRECTED (Stage 3): UNVERIFIED — 'Malaysia banned US warships' → 'Putrajaya was reported to stop accepting port-of-call applications from US warships' (secondary reporting attribution).
CORRECTED (Stage 3): INCORRECT — '~2,000 ships are blocked globally' → 'around 20,000 seafarers were reported trapped in the Persian Gulf' (the UN/IMO figure).
REPHRASED (Stage 3): UNVERIFIED — '90% traffic drop' qualified to 'industry reporting cited'.
REPHRASED (Stage 3): MISLEADING — 'Japan and South Korea built institutional Gulf presence' more carefully scoped (Korea Cheonghae verified in Gulf of Aden; Japan SDF excludes Hormuz).

REPHRASED (Stage 3): UNVERIFIED — View 'Malaysia chose neither, gap showed' → 'CMF membership without operational presence, plus reliance on a direct PM call, are the structural features the episode exposed'.

PRESERVED (Stage 3 VERIFIED): seven Malaysian-company tankers; vessel breakdown 3 Petronas/2 MISC/1 Sapura/1 Sepadu; Malaysia not on Iran's safe list; Iran's safe list named countries; Brent $126/barrel; RM4B/month subsidy bill; South Korea Cheonghae in Gulf of Aden; CMF Malaysian membership.
